Motion For Publication
Off Calendar Jan-23-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Theresa Murphy (415-678-5927) plaintiff and moving party in Pro Per; No other appearance

Court finds that plaintiff did not comply with the Tentative Ruling Rule and is not permitted to speak. Plaintiff also seeks clarification of the court's ruling. Court finds that plaintiff is actually seeking advice and plaintiff is directed to seek legal assistance.

The tentative ruling is adopted as follows: PLAINTIFF THERESA MURPHY's Motion For Publication is denied, improperly noticed in Department 501 San Francisco Local Rule 3.1(c)(2). Prevailing party to prepare order. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Smith, Not reported (D501)

Motion For Pendency Of Action
Off Calendar Jan-23-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Theresa Murphy (415-678-5927) plaintiff and moving party in Pro Per; No other appearance

Court finds that plaintiff did not comply with the Tentative Ruling Rule and is not permitted to speak. Plaintiff also seeks clarification of the court's ruling. Court finds that plaintiff is actually seeking advice and plaintiff is directed to seek legal assistance.

The tentative ruling is adopted as follows: PLAINTIFF THERESA MURPHY's Motion For Pendency Of Action is denied, failed to name and serve all entities claiming interest in the premises. Prevailing party to prepare order. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Smith, Not reported (D501)

Motion For Service On Secretary Of State
Off Calendar Jan-23-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Theresa Murphy (415-678-5927) plaintiff and moving party in Pro Per; No other appearance

Court finds that plaintiff did not comply with the Tentative Ruling Rule and is not permitted to speak. Plaintiff also seeks clarification of the court's ruling. Court finds that plaintiff is actually seeking advice and plaintiff is directed to seek legal assistance.

The tentative ruling is adopted as follows: PLAINTIFF THERESA MURPHY's Motion For Service On Secretary Of State is denied, no admissable evidence to show Pro Funding is dissolved and or that William Coleman is and "officer, director or person" in charge of Pro Funding.
